compiled_state.title: ticket show output is invalid JSON when comments contain embedded JSON payloads
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Expected Behavior
ticket show should produce valid JSON output that can be parsed by json.loads() or jq without error, regardless of comment content.

## Actual Behavior
When a ticket has comments containing embedded JSON (e.g., PREPLANNING_CONTEXT payloads), the ticket show output becomes invalid JSON because the inner JSON is not properly escaped within the comment body string. This forces consumers to use regex-based extraction instead of standard JSON parsing.

## Reproduction
1. Create a ticket with a comment containing a JSON payload (e.g., PREPLANNING_CONTEXT)
2. Run .claude/scripts/dso ticket show <id>
3. Attempt to parse the output with python3 -c "import json,sys; json.load(sys.stdin)"
4. Observe parse error due to unescaped inner JSON

## Impact
sprint-drift-check.sh _check_relates_to_drift() had to use regex extraction instead of json.loads() to handle epic ticket data. Any future script that parses ticket show output with a JSON parser will encounter the same issue.

comments.body: Investigated: The canonical ticket comment path (ticket-comment.sh → python3 json.dump) properly escapes embedded JSON. The bug likely comes from scripts writing COMMENT event files directly via shell printf/echo, bypassing ticket-comment.sh. Need to grep for direct event file writes that skip Python serialization. The canonical paths (ticket-show.sh, ticket-reducer.py) are not vulnerable.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
Investigation results: Cannot reproduce. Root cause investigation:

1. ticket-comment.sh writes body to a temp file then serializes via python3 json.dump(ensure_ascii=False) — this properly escapes ALL special chars including embedded JSON quotes.
2. ticket-reducer.py uses json.dumps(ensure_ascii=False) for all state output — valid per RFC 8259.
3. ticket-show.sh uses json.dumps(json.load(...), ensure_ascii=False) — re-serializes the already-valid reducer output.
4. End-to-end test: created ticket, added PREPLANNING_CONTEXT comment with embedded JSON (quotes, newlines), ran ticket show — output passed python3 json.loads() without error.

Hypothesis: The sprint-drift-check.sh comment ('may contain invalid JSON') was added defensively before ticket-comment.sh adopted the temp-file body approach (preventing ARG_MAX truncation of large payloads). With the temp-file approach in place, the canonical write path is safe.

No code change identified as necessary. sprint-drift-check.sh regex workaround remains as defensive measure. Leaving ticket open pending user review of whether workaround should be reverted or confirmed as permanent.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
Investigation findings (2026-04-09): The reported symptom — invalid JSON output when comment bodies contain embedded JSON — CANNOT be reproduced with current code. json.dumps() correctly double-escapes embedded JSON strings; round-trip via json.loads() preserves the body exactly in all tested scenarios (PREPLANNING_CONTEXT payloads, gate signal JSON, unicode content). The bash echo pipeline in ticket-show.sh also handles backslash sequences correctly.

hypothesis_tests:
- hypothesis: json.dumps output becomes invalid JSON when body contains embedded JSON → DISPROVED
- hypothesis: ticket-show.sh echo pipeline corrupts embedded JSON characters → DISPROVED
- hypothesis: U+2028/U+2029 in bodies causes multi-line output that breaks parsers → INCONCLUSIVE (technically valid per RFC 8259 but non-standard)
- hypothesis: No existing test covers embedded-JSON-in-comments case → CONFIRMED

Two latent issues identified and tracked separately (see linked tickets):
1. Missing regression test for embedded JSON in comment bodies
2. Latent type-safety risk: COMMENT event body field is never validated as a string — if bridge-inbound.py returns Jira ADF body as a dict, it would silently produce broken string-parsing downstream without any error

Leaving open per Rule 19 (no code change made). Latent issues filed separately.
comments.author: Test
